hand_profile.script_style: Humanistic cursive with elements of Spanish documentary hand, typical of 17th-century notarial and legal manuscripts; moderately sloped, with fluid, connected strokes and some flourished ascenders and descenders.

letter_formations.u_v: Generally distinguishable; 'u' is rounded and open at the bottom, while 'v' has a pointed bottom and is more angular.
letter_formations.f_s: Distinguishable; 'f' has a crossbar and descends below the baseline, while 's' appears in both long and round forms.
letter_formations.s: Both long-s (ſ) and round-s are present; long-s is used medially, round-s at the end of words.
letter_formations.r: Straight r is predominant, with occasional use of a more rounded or slightly rotunda form.
letter_formations.a: Single-compartment, open at the top right, sometimes with a slight flourish.
letter_formations.e: Small, open, with a distinct loop at the top, often connected to the following letter.

hand_profile.marginalia_position: upper left margin, running parallel to the main text block
special_challenges: tight word spacing causing letter crowding
flourished ascenders and descenders obscure adjacent lines
curator_rules: u and v are used interchangeably
f and s are used interchangeably
ç old spelling is always modern z
accents are inconsistent — transcribe exactly as written
horizontal cap over letter means n follows
long-s and round-s both present — transcribe as written
abbreviation marks: transcribe the expansion not the symbol
